root_text.sn29.7:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn29.7:1.3: “ko nu kho, bhante, hetu, ko paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti?
root_text.sn29.7:2.1: “Idha, bhikkhu, ekacco kāyena dvayakārī hoti, vācāya dvayakārī hoti, manasā dvayakārī hoti.
root_text.sn29.7:2.5: ‘aho vatāhaṁ kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjeyyan’ti.
root_text.sn29.7:2.6: So kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.sn29.7:2.7: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhu, hetu, ayaṁ paccayo, yena midhekacco kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā aṇḍajānaṁ nāgānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjatī”ti.

translation_text.sn29.7:1.2: Seated to one side, that mendicant said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn29.7:1.3: “Sir, what is the cause, what is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the egg-born dragons?”
translation_text.sn29.7:2.1: “Mendicant, it’s when someone does both kinds of deeds by body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.sn29.7:2.3: ‘The egg-born dragons are long-lived, beautiful, and very happy.’
translation_text.sn29.7:2.5: ‘If only, when my body breaks up, after death, I would be reborn in the company of the egg-born dragons!’
translation_text.sn29.7:2.6: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in the company of the egg-born dragons.
translation_text.sn29.7:2.7: This is the cause, this is the reason why someone, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in the company of the egg-born dragons.”
